metadata.title: Why the Turkey's Model Was Perfect Until Thanksgiving
metadata.purpose: vox-explainer slate cut of Computational Skepticism candidate 07. Source chapter: books/computational-skepticism-for-ai/chapters/14-the-limits-of-ai-what-the-tools-cannot-do.md (Limit 3: the data-world gap; Taleb's turkey problem).
metadata.clock: narration (ElevenLabs) — durations below are word-count estimates until GATE 0 audio lock

style_bible.visual_style: editorial paper collage on real newsprint scan; desaturated plates; flat annotation plane; serif labels with hairline underlines; hand-drawn annotation ring used once per film
style_bible.color_palette: cream ground, charcoal serif type, terracotta = the turkey's/model's confidence (the picture built from the data), dusty blue = the world / the true risk, gold highlighter reserved for short single-line quotes only

metadata.color_semantics: terracotta #D35F43 = confidence, the model's picture built from its data; dusty blue #5B7B9C = the world's actual risk; never swap mid-film
metadata.note: Card exclusions (candidate 07): no Mediocristan/Extremistan taxonomy, no Hume/induction history, no distribution-shift formalism, no medical-AI second example — one turkey, one curve, one cliff. Both quote cards carry wrapped lines: NO gold sweeps this film (Gate B lesson — sweeps only on short single-line quotes). One HandRing: B05, on the confidence peak. Taleb quote wording to be verified verbatim at factcheck before render (Gate F).
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: B01: palette=claude but the cold open is 'un-annotated' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B11: palette=claude but the outro is 'un-annotated' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. A model whose evidence got better every single day — right up to the morning it failed completely. This is the turkey problem. Your AI may have it.

card.copy: The turkey's model was perfect — until Thanksgiving

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [TITLE] the turkey's model was perfect

beats.narration_text: A turkey is fed every morning. The same generous hand, the same reliable grain. A thousand mornings in a row.
beats.scene_description: an archival-feeling farmyard plate — one turkey at morning feeding, grain scattered — slow push-in on the turkey
beats.image_prompt: archival farm photograph, a single turkey in a farmyard at morning feeding time, scattered grain on the ground, a hand offering feed at the frame edge, desaturated flat print reproduction pinned like a clipping to aged newsprint, editorial paper collage, no readable text

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [PHOTO] the fed turkey, push-in

beats.narration_text: Each morning is a data point. Every single one supports the same comfortable model: the human cares about my welfare. No exceptions on record.

production_viz.label: a thousand mornings of evidence
production_viz.mechanic: isotype grid fills in reading order — square day-marks accumulating row by row with a mono counter climbing to 1,000; declared unit chip: '■ = 10 mornings' (100 marks = 1,000 mornings); a serif model-card floats above the grid: 'the model: the human cares about my welfare'
production_viz.note: unit scaling declared on screen — isotype one-unit-per-mark honesty preserved (1 mark = 10 mornings, stated)

beats.narration_text: Plot the turkey's confidence in that model. By every measure it can check — food, shelter, safety — the line climbs. More evidence, more confidence. Never one bad day.

production_viz.label: confidence, day 1 to day 1,000
production_viz.mechanic: axes draw in (days 0–1,000 × confidence, zero-based); the terracotta confidence curve draws on left to right, rising monotonically; axis label boxes float clear of the strokes with tick connectors
production_viz.note: monotone rise is the claim — no dips anywhere; zero baseline

beats.narration_text: By day one thousand, confidence has never been higher. Look where that maximum lands on the calendar: the day before Thanksgiving.
beats.scene_description: the curve persists; the editor's pen draws one terracotta ring around the peak at day 1,000 — the film's single ring — while a calendar chip lands beneath it: 'day 1,000 — the day before Thanksgiving'

production_viz.mechanic: HandRing on the curve's final point; LabelChip 'day 1,000 — the day before Thanksgiving' placed clear of the curve
production_viz.note: one ring only — the film's single ring

beats.narration_text: Day one thousand and one. The model fails, completely and at once. The evidence was never wrong — it was all drawn from inside the smooth days.

production_viz.label: day 1,001
production_viz.mechanic: the same curve extends one day past the ringed peak and drops vertically to zero — the cliff drawn fast; a charcoal serif label 'day 1,001' at the drop, floated clear of the stroke
production_viz.note: the drop is drawn, not faded — the failure is an event, not a decay

beats.narration_text: Taleb, who told this story, put the cruelty plainly: the feeling of safety and the actual danger peaked at exactly the same moment.

document.quote: the feeling of safety reached its maximum when the risk was at the highest
document.attribution: — Nassim Nicholas Taleb, The Black Swan (2007)

beats.scene_description: quote card, charcoal serif on cream; wrapped lines so NO gold sweep (Gate B lesson); fragment of Taleb's sentence 'Consider that the feeling of safety reached its maximum when the risk was at the highest!' — verified at factcheck via secondary sources, confirm against print before publication
beats.new_visual_element: [QUOTE] safety at maximum, risk at highest

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [QUOTE] safety at maximum, risk at highest

beats.narration_text: Now swap the turkey for an AI system. Tested on ten thousand routine cases: ninety-nine point seven percent accurate. It may still be a turkey.

production_viz.label: ten thousand routine cases
production_viz.mechanic: isotype grid of square case-marks (declared unit chip: '■ = 100 cases'; 100 marks = 10,000 cases) fills in reading order in dusty blue; a chip lands: '99.7% accurate'; then OUTSIDE the grid's frame one empty outlined slot draws in, labeled 'the case that ends the regime — not in the data'
production_viz.note: the empty slot sits outside the grid boundary — outside-the-data is the geometry of the claim

beats.new_visual_element: [ISOTYPE] ten thousand routine cases + the empty slot

beats.narration_text: And more data cannot fix it. The model's competence is over its data. The event that ends the smooth regime left no trace there to learn.

production_viz.label: confidence vs. the world
production_viz.mechanic: shared axes; the terracotta confidence line draws on, climbing; above it a flat dusty-blue line draws on — 'the world's risk — never moved'; label boxes float clear of both strokes with tick connectors; the vertical gap between the lines closes as confidence climbs toward a risk it cannot see
production_viz.note: the blue line is flat by design: the risk was constant, only the confidence moved — divergence of belief from world, not a moving hazard

beats.new_visual_element: [CHART] confidence climbs toward a risk it cannot see

beats.narration_text: The rule survives past poultry: the system's competence is over its data, not the world. Accuracy on the cases it has seen is a fact about the seen.

document.quote: The system's competence is over the data, not the world.

beats.scene_description: quote card, charcoal serif on cream; wraps to two lines so NO gold sweep (Gate B lesson)

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [QUOTE] competence is over the data

beats.narration_text: So when a system's confidence has never been higher, ask the turkey's question: is the world really smooth — or has Thanksgiving simply not arrived yet?

card.copy: The data is always less than the world.
card.sub: from Computational Skepticism for AI — chapter 14

beats.new_visual_element: [CARD] endcard — the data is less than the world

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [CARD] endcard — the data is less than the world
